title: AI Cultivation World Simulator
subtitle: AI Cultivation World Simulator

phase.parsing_data: Parsing Laws of Heaven and Earth

tips: Modifying character goals can change their behavior style.
Character traits greatly influence their behavior style.
Cultivating in a cave dwelling matching your spiritual root doubles efficiency.
Heavenly Spiritual Root characters have double efficiency in any cave dwelling.
Changing world spirit density affects bonuses and subtly adjusts character behavior.
Every character has their own real thoughts and emotions.
Besides cultivation, alchemy and qi refinement are also important.
Participating in auctions may yield bargains, but beware of villains chasing you.
Jianghu peers will give you a nickname based on your actions.
Dual cultivation is good, but please exercise restraint.
Recovering at the sect headquarters can fully restore HP.
The gap between realms is immense; challenging a higher realm is as hard as ascending to heaven.
Characters with the Child of Destiny trait have continuous good luck and fortuitous encounters.
Modern world transmigrators only want to return to reality, but it is impossible.
Elixirs have a time limit for their effects.
Because the LLM needs to think, game startup may take a long time.
Simulating the world consumes a lot of LLM tokens, please be aware.
Setting history at the start will change the entire cultivation world accordingly.
Treasures won at auctions can greatly enhance your strength, but save your spirit stones.
At a bottleneck, years of effort may still lose to a single moment of insight.
The higher the realm, the more perilous the breakthrough; impatience harms the foundation.
Seclusion is not escape from the world, but a wager of years against fate.
A cave dwelling that matches your root can outweigh ten years of bitter cultivation.
Good cave dwellings are seldom given; more often, they are fought over.
Sect grounds are fit for recovery, not reckless bravado.
To fight again before your wounds close is often to court another defeat.
Cross-realm battles are won by more than courage alone.
Techniques restrain one another; know the opponent's path before trading blows.
Even a divine weapon takes time to learn in the hand.
With spirit stones in hand, opportunities are more willing to speak.
The costliest item in the auction hall is not always the worthiest.
Elixirs are wondrous, but greed can still injure the body.
Some medicinal force lingers long and should not be taken again too soon.
Meditation values stillness of heart; in stillness, insight comes on its own.
Body Tempering is the bluntest path, and often the steadiest.
Where fellow disciples gather is often better for cultivation than the wilds.
Dual cultivation follows affinity; when forced, it often breeds trouble.
Fortuitous encounters favor those who are prepared.
The farther a nickname spreads, the faster trouble tends to follow.
An occupied Cave Dwelling and an unclaimed one often lead to two very different fates of cultivation.
Outside the Sect, one finds opportunities; within the Sect, one finds stability.
Staying in seclusion too long does not always mean true progress.
Once your approach is seen through, even the finest Cultivation Technique will lose its edge.
Without enough Spirit Stones, many opportunities cannot even be approached.
When an Elixir suits you, it helps; when it does not, it becomes torment.
For a Rogue Cultivator, the hardest part is not cultivation, but having no one to fall back on.
True treasures are the quickest to draw the covetous eyes of others.
Meditation seeks stillness of heart, not blind haste.
In guiding others, one is often cultivating oneself as well.
Body Tempering may look crude, yet it often reveals the strongest foundation.
Different Orthodoxies walk different paths of cultivation.
Not every path is suited to Respire, and not every cultivator is suited to silent, secluded training.
Some Orthodoxies refine the heart, while others refine the body.
Walking the path that suits you matters more than blindly copying others.
